concerns.text: FR-009 mandates 'Benjamini-Hochberg multiple-comparison correction'. The plan explicitly rejects this for 'Maximum Statistic', and tasks T032/T039 implement Maximum Statistic. No task exists to update the spec (FR-009) to reflect this methodological change, leaving the spec requirement unimplemented and contradictory to the plan.

US-3 Acceptance Scenario 4 requires 'power validation' (≥80% detection rate) for injected anisotropy. While T033 implements 'power validation logic', there is no task to generate the power curve report or document the detection rate results required to satisfy the acceptance scenario's verification step.

Task T039 description states 'reporting of both uncorrected and multiple-comparison corrected p-values'. This phrasing implies the Benjamini-Hochberg method (from the spec) rather than the 'Maximum Statistic' method (from the plan). The task description should be updated to explicitly name 'Maximum Statistic' to align with the implemented logic in T032.
